title: Numbered Doors
shortDescription: Four interconnected hallways of randomized numbered doors leading all across the Backrooms, around an unpredictable hub and a deadly exit passage.
atmosphere: Maze-like and disorienting, with passageways that shift and mysterious numbered doors breeding constant uncertainty.
tips: Trust labeled doors 105–356 for known destinations. Avoid unlabeled or 'trustworthy'-claiming doors. Never open the red Exit door.

title: Ruins Left Behind
shortDescription: A collapsed multi-storey car park once held by the Emstable micronation, now hazardous ruins of unstable structure and deep mining pits.
atmosphere: Desolate industrial decay: rubble, rusted vehicles, abandoned tents and the skeletal remains of a failed society in the dark.
tips: Beware collapse and deep-pit falls. Traversal is extremely dangerous; most wanderers avoid this level entirely.

title: The Petrified Garden
shortDescription: A planetoid-sized superorganism of interwoven ancient trees, with bioluminescent caverns and ruins that mimic classical Frontrooms structures.
atmosphere: Dense shadowy canopy in perpetual twilight; underground glow-rooms pulse with eerie light amid petrified wood and forgotten ruins.
tips: Never say 'it's only a matter of time' — it wakes buried sentries. Shelter at Base Seedling, which holds the main exit. Don't harm the Green Giants.

title: The Moon
shortDescription: A giant plastic scale model of the solar system set in a Victorian study; only the Moon's surface, coated in toxic lead paint, is reachable.
atmosphere: A vast barren expanse of grey lead paint on a plastic moon, swept by toxic mists and sandstorms, an immense humanoid sometimes crossing it.
tips: Stay clear of weather events. Limit exposure to paint dust and fumes. Follow PlasticWorks miners for safe exit routes.

title: The Quarter Hub
shortDescription: A dusty abandoned arcade spanning miles whose broken cabinets act as interdimensional transit hubs between Backrooms levels.
atmosphere: Desolate echoing arcade thick with dust and decay, vintage machines vandalized by age, a faint electrical hum in the silence.
tips: Find a working cabinet and insert quarters to travel. Only ~1 in 1500 works. An arcade always appears when you arrive.

title: The SS Fun =)
shortDescription: A corrupted cruise ship — Partygoer headquarters — that randomly no-clips between levels, its infinite interior full of party horrors.
atmosphere: A grotesque unending celebration laced with danger: party rooms below contaminated decks, screams echoing through shifting corridors.
tips: Avoid all party paraphernalia. Touch a reversed, unfocused poster to escape. Never board willingly — the ship reappears unpredictably.

title: The Bunker Springs
shortDescription: A small cave with a mineral-rich hot spring that soothes and heals wanderers — while hiding a darker secret further in.
atmosphere: Serene and therapeutic at first, concealing an unsettling truth about the level's true nature and its edges.
tips: Bathe 1–2 hours at most for the benefits. Avoid the second waterfall tunnel. Respect others and leave promptly.

title: Final Virtue
shortDescription: Windswept grasslands under permanent overcast around a medieval castle, where a sentient black tar slowly creeps in from the mountains.
atmosphere: Melancholic and desolate; a blue glow lights empty fields where a lone armored figure holds back an advancing darkness.
tips: Avoid the tar-like abyss at all costs. The Blue Knight is friendly but clingy. Lightning strikes hourly in predictable zones.

title: Hyperian
shortDescription: A vast island civilization of 25,000 across 250 societies, ringed by mountains and anomalous oceans full of dangerous entities.
atmosphere: An island sanctuary of thriving settlements and strange flora, offset by deadly seas and an ancient sleeping leviathan.
tips: Arrive via the dockyards and befriend settlement reps. Never travel the ocean — the waters and the Kraken make escape near-impossible.

title: Shifted Beyond Reality
shortDescription: A reality-warping skybox of floating islands where Memory Lurkers bend perception and cognition, trapping wanderers in false sensations.
atmosphere: Perpetual daylight with dreamlike distortion; everything feels artificial and flat, steeped in a constant sense of unreality.
tips: Avoid Memory Lurkers — their touch corrupts the mind and fakes safety. Trust your original memories and question what you perceive.
